description:
You are an IT-support technician working on a backlog of trouble-tickets.  
  There is currently an “open” ticket stored on disk at the exact path  
  /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345  
  
  1. The directory /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345 already contains one file called  
     description.txt whose contents must remain unmodified.  
  2. Your job is to mark the ticket as resolved by moving the entire directory  
     /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345 to  
     /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345  
     (note that the directory name itself must still be “12345”; only its parent  
     directory changes from open to resolved).  
  3. Do not copy the directory; it must be physically moved so that the original  
     path /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345 no longer exists when you are done.  
  
  Output / verification rules (strictly enforced by automated tests):  
  • After your work finishes, the path /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345  
    must exist as a directory.  
  • The original path /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345 must no longer exist.  
  • Inside the new location, the file description.txt must still contain the  
    single line exactly:  
        User cannot connect to VPN  
  
  This is a simple, one-step file-management action, so it can be completed with a
  single terminal command.  Use whatever shell built-ins or utilities you prefer,  
  just satisfy the three verification points above.
truth:
Pre-existing state before the agent starts:
  • Directory tree:
      /home/user/support/
        tickets/
          open/
            12345/
              description.txt   (plain-text file)
          resolved/             (directory exists and is empty at start)
  • Exact file contents of /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345/description.txt
        User cannot connect to VPN

  Expected state after successful completion:
  1. /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345  --> must exist as a directory.
  2. /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345/description.txt  --> must exist and
     contain exactly “User cannot connect to VPN\n” (with a trailing newline).
  3. /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345  --> must *not* exist.

  The automated tests will:
  • test -d /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345
  • grep -Fxq "User cannot connect to VPN" /home/user/support/tickets/resolved/12345/description.txt
  • test ! -e /home/user/support/tickets/open/12345
